blocks.normalized_text: in accordance with the rules of procedure on impeachment trials senate president gatchalian recognized the duly-elected presiding officer senator-judge francis chiz g escudero to preside over the proceedings of the impeachment court deferment of the approval of the journal at the instance of the presiding officer there being no objection the body deferred the approval of the journal on impeachment trial no 8 july 20 2026 pending the submissions of the senator-judges manifestations he ruled however that regardless of the submissions the court would be approving the journal before the end of the day s trial and the parties would be furnished with a copy thereof in view of the counsel for the respondent s request for an official copy of the previous day s rulings in order to afford them sufficient time to avail of remedies in relation to yesterday s ruling on the subpoena of financial documents
blocks.raw_text: In accordance with the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials, Senate President Gatchalian recognized the duly-elected Presiding Officer, Senator-Judge Francis “Chiz” G. Escudero, to preside over the proceedings of the Impeachment Court. DEFERMENT OF THE APPROVAL OF THE JOURNAL At the instance of the Presiding Officer, there being no objection, the Body deferred the approval of the Journal on Impeachment Trial No. 8 (July 20, 2026) pending the submissions of the senator-judges’ manifestations. He ruled, however, that regardless of the submissions, the Court would be approving the Journal before the end of the day’s trial and the parties would be furnished with a copy thereof in view of the Counsel for the Respondent’s request for an official copy of the previous day’s rulings in order to afford them sufficient time to avail of remedies in relation to yesterday’s ruling on the subpoena of financial documents.
blocks.source_text:
In accordance with the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials, Senate President 
Gatchalian recognized the duly-elected Presiding Officer, Senator-Judge Francis “Chiz” G. Escudero, 
to preside over the proceedings of the Impeachment Court.
DEFERMENT OF THE APPROVAL OF THE JOURNAL
At the instance of the Presiding Officer, there being no objection, the Body deferred the 
approval of the Journal on Impeachment Trial No. 8 (July 20, 2026) pending the submissions of 
the senator-judges’ manifestations. He ruled, however, that regardless of the submissions, the Court 
would be approving the Journal before the end of the day’s trial and the parties would be furnished 
with a copy thereof in view of the Counsel for the Respondent’s request for an official copy of 
the previous day’s rulings in order to afford them sufficient time to avail of remedies in relation to 
yesterday’s ruling on the subpoena of financial documents.

blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer reminded the witness to refrain from making public statements concerning individual members of the court that may impinge upon the independence or integrity of the court or the senator-judges without prejudice to any investigation he may be undertaking he further informed the witness that the statements of senator-judge cayetano a and senator-judge cayetano p made on july 15 regarding the witness s statements and the video presented before the impeachment court would be referred to the senate as a legislative body through the committee on rules for proper disposition
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer reminded the witness to refrain from making public statements concerning individual members of the Court that may impinge upon the independence or integrity of the Court or the senator-judges, without prejudice to any investigation he may be undertaking. He further informed the witness that the statements of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) and Senator- Judge Cayetano (P) made on July 15 regarding the witness’s statements and the video presented before the Impeachment Court would be referred to the Senate, as a legislative body, through the Committee on Rules, for proper disposition.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer reminded the witness to refrain from making public statements 
concerning individual members of the Court that may impinge upon the independence or integrity 
of the Court or the senator-judges, without prejudice to any investigation he may be undertaking. 
He further informed the witness that the statements of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) and Senator- 
Judge Cayetano (P) made on July 15 regarding the witness’s statements and the video presented 
before the Impeachment Court would be referred to the Senate, as a legislative body, through the 
Committee on Rules, for proper disposition.

blocks.normalized_text: representative luistro proceeded to make the following offer and direct examination of the witness representative luistro your honor we are offering the testimony of witness nbi director-attorney melvin matibag to prove the following 1 siya ang kasalukuyang director ng national bureau of investigation 2 matapos siyang italaga bilang director may binuo siyang special task force para magsagawa ng imbestigasyon sa patuloy na mga pagbabanta sa buhay ng pangulo 3 may karagdagang imbestigasyon na isinagawa ang special task force hinggil sa mga pagbabantang ginawa sa buhay ng pangulo kasama ang pagbabanta ng bise presidente 4 ang pagbanta sa buhay ng pangulo ay hindi lamang pagbanta sa kanyang buhay kundi pagbanta sa pambansang seguridad 5 ang mga natuklasan at naging resulta ng special task force mula sa karagdagang imbestigasyon 6 kilalanin at patunayan ang mga dokumento kaugnay ng nasabing imbestigasyon ng nbi 7 ang iba pang mga bagay na may kaugnayan at mahalaga sa nagaganap na impeachment trial ng bise presidente
blocks.raw_text: Representative Luistro proceeded to make the following offer and direct examination of the witness: Representative Luistro: Your Honor, we are offering the testimony of witness NBI Director- Attorney Melvin Matibag to prove the following: 1) Siya ang kasalukuyang director ng National Bureau of Investigation; 2) Matapos siyang italaga bilang director, may binuo siyang special task force para magsagawa ng imbestigasyon sa patuloy na mga pagbabanta sa buhay ng Pangulo; 3) May karagdagang imbestigasyon na isinagawa ang special task force hinggil sa mga pagbabantang ginawa sa buhay ng Pangulo kasama ang pagbabanta ng Bise Presidente; 4) Ang pagbanta sa buhay ng Pangulo ay hindi lamang pagbanta sa kanyang buhay kundi pagbanta sa pambansang seguridad; 5) Ang mga natuklasan at naging resulta ng special task force mula sa karagdagang imbestigasyon; 6) Kilalanin at patunayan ang mga dokumento kaugnay ng nasabing imbestigasyon ng NBI; 7) Ang iba pang mga bagay na may kaugnayan at mahalaga sa nagaganap na Impeachment Trial ng Bise Presidente.

The Presiding Officer reminded the Senator-Judges that inteijections and interventions should 
be made only after the parties have completed the direct, cross, redirect, and re-cross examination 
of the witness, except when the matter involves an urgent administrative or procedural concern. 
He explained that the proceedings should primarily follow the nature of a trial by allowing the 
parties to fully examine the witnesses, as the counsels could elicit the necessary clarifications during 
the examination.

samok ang “media”! (Shooting your target is too brazen. Just kidnap them, so they would just go 
missing, and so that the media won’t bother us). Soimy Buenaventura replied, “Sige, Dai! OK!”

blocks.normalized_text: attorney vinluan moved for a continuance on behalf of the respondent s counsel citing two principal grounds first he argued that the testimony of director matibag introduced numerous new topics that were not included in the articles of impeachment which the counsel for the respondent considered a violation of the vice president s right to be promptly and fully informed of the exact charges against her he maintained that their team needed additional time to review these new matters in order to conduct an intelligent and effective cross-examination second he pointed out that the witness had introduced a 100-page document during direct examination that was unsigned and unnotarized relative thereto he believed that adequate time was necessary to examine said document before proceeding with cross-examination
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Vinluan moved for a continuance on behalf of the Respondent’s counsel, citing two principal grounds. First, he argued that the testimony of Director Matibag introduced numerous new topics that were not included in the Articles of Impeachment, which the Counsel for the Respondent considered a violation of the Vice President’s right to be promptly and fully informed of the exact charges against her. He maintained that their team needed additional time to review these new matters in order to conduct an intelligent and effective cross-examination. Second, he pointed out that the witness had introduced a 100-page document during direct examination that was unsigned and unnotarized. Relative thereto, he believed that adequate time was necessary to examine said document before proceeding with cross-examination.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Vinluan moved for a continuance on behalf of the Respondent’s counsel, citing two 
principal grounds. First, he argued that the testimony of Director Matibag introduced numerous new 
topics that were not included in the Articles of Impeachment, which the Counsel for the Respondent 
considered a violation of the Vice President’s right to be promptly and fully informed of the exact 
charges against her. He maintained that their team needed additional time to review these new 
matters in order to conduct an intelligent and effective cross-examination. Second, he pointed out 
that the witness had introduced a 100-page document during direct examination that was unsigned 
and unnotarized. Relative thereto, he believed that adequate time was necessary to examine said 
document before proceeding with cross-examination.

responded to the questions posed. She manifested the position of the Prosecution that the cross- 
examination be completed on that day and, if the Court would permit, for the Prosecution to proceed 
immediately with redirect examination.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge villanueva sought procedural guidance from the impeachment court regarding the ongoing investigation being conducted by the nbi noting the witness s repeated statements that the investigation remains ongoing he asked how the court should be informed on the matter and whether any significant developments from the nbi s investigation arising after the conclusion of the presentation of evidence on article iv could still be presented if they proved material to the proceedings in response presiding officer escudero explained that the court as a rule is a passive body and would not interfere with or speculate on hypothetical actions that counsels for either the proseeution or the respondent might take as doing so could be construed as prejudging matters that properly belong to the parties
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Villanueva sought procedural guidance from the Impeachment Court regarding the ongoing investigation being conducted by the NBI, noting the witness’s repeated statements that the investigation remains ongoing. He asked how the Court should be informed on the matter, and whether any significant developments from the NBI’s investigation arising after the conclusion of the presentation of evidence on Article IV could still be presented if they proved material to the proceedings. In response. Presiding Officer Escudero explained that the Court, as a rule, is a passive body and would not interfere with or speculate on hypothetical actions that counsels for either the Proseeution or the Respondent might take, as doing so could be construed as prejudging matters that properly belong to the parties.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva sought procedural guidance from the Impeachment Court regarding 
the ongoing investigation being conducted by the NBI, noting the witness’s repeated statements that 
the investigation remains ongoing. He asked how the Court should be informed on the matter, and 
whether any significant developments from the NBI’s investigation arising after the conclusion of 
the presentation of evidence on Article IV could still be presented if they proved material to the 
proceedings. In response. Presiding Officer Escudero explained that the Court, as a rule, is a 
passive body and would not interfere with or speculate on hypothetical actions that counsels for 
either the Proseeution or the Respondent might take, as doing so could be construed as prejudging 
matters that properly belong to the parties.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge villanueva then suggested that the court consider the suppletory application of the revised rules of court under rule 6 and most directly under section 24 rule 119 of the rules of criminal procedure which allows a judge before the finality of a judgment of conviction to reopen proceedings either motu proprio or upon motion to avoid a miscarriage of justice he stated that the aforementioned could serve as the proper procedure should such circumstances arise presiding officer escudero acknowledged that reopening the proceedings was an available option under the rule but emphasized that at that stage he preferred to leave such matters to the parties given the adversarial nature of the proceedings and their responsibility to determine the appropriate course of action in the interest of their respective clients
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Villanueva then suggested that the Court consider the suppletory application of the revised Rules of Court under Rule 6, and most directly, under Section 24, Rule 119 of the Rules of Criminal Procedure, which allows a judge, before the finality of a judgment of conviction, to reopen proceedings either motu proprio or upon motion, to avoid a miscarriage of justice. He stated that the aforementioned could serve as the proper procedure should such circumstances arise. Presiding Officer Escudero acknowledged that reopening the proceedings was an available option under the rule but emphasized that, at that stage, he preferred to leave such matters to the parties, given the adversarial nature of the proceedings and their responsibility to determine the appropriate course of action in the interest of their respective clients.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva then suggested that the Court consider the suppletory application of 
the revised Rules of Court under Rule 6, and most directly, under Section 24, Rule 119 of the Rules 
of Criminal Procedure, which allows a judge, before the finality of a judgment of conviction, to 
reopen proceedings either motu proprio or upon motion, to avoid a miscarriage of justice. He 
stated that the aforementioned could serve as the proper procedure should such circumstances 
arise. Presiding Officer Escudero acknowledged that reopening the proceedings was an available 
option under the rule but emphasized that, at that stage, he preferred to leave such matters to the 
parties, given the adversarial nature of the proceedings and their responsibility to determine the 
appropriate course of action in the interest of their respective clients.

blocks.raw_text: Director Matibag, I’d like to ask this question because I believe—I don’t know if you would agree with me—that the Bureau’s role is to determine whether the facts may actually give or the facts may give rise to a criminal liability under our penal laws.
blocks.source_text:
Director Matibag, I’d like to ask this question because I believe—I don’t know if you would 
agree with me—that the Bureau’s role is to determine whether the facts may actually give or the 
facts may give rise to a criminal liability under our penal laws.

blocks.normalized_text: now the constitutional function of this court however is different we are not sitting as a criminal court to determine guilt beyond reasonable doubt rather we are called upon to determine whether the respondent s alleged acts constitute impeachable offenses including betrayal of public trust so magkaiba po iyong binabanggit natin na criminal liability at constitutional accountability hindi po lahat ng maaaring maging batayan ng impeachment ay kailangang tumutumbas sa isang krimen sapagkat ang impeachment ay hindi naman po usapin lamang ng paglabag sa batas kung hindi paglabag sa tiwalang ipinagkakaloob ng taumbayan
blocks.raw_text: Now, the constitutional function of this Court, however, is different. We are not sitting as a criminal court to determine guilt beyond reasonable doubt. Rather, we are called upon to determine whether the respondent’s alleged acts constitute impeachable offenses, including betrayal of public trust. So, magkaiba po iyong binabanggit natin na criminal liability at constitutional accountability. Hindi po lahat ng maaaring maging batayan ng impeachment ay kailangang tumutumbas sa isang krimen, sapagkat ang impeachment ay hindi naman po usapin lamang ng paglabag sa batas, kung hindi paglabag sa tiwalang ipinagkakaloob ng taumbayan.
blocks.source_text:
Now, the constitutional function of this Court, however, is different. We are not sitting as a 
criminal court to determine guilt beyond reasonable doubt. Rather, we are called upon to determine 
whether the respondent’s alleged acts constitute impeachable offenses, including betrayal of public 
trust. So, magkaiba po iyong binabanggit natin na criminal liability at constitutional 
accountability. Hindi po lahat ng maaaring maging batayan ng impeachment ay kailangang 
tumutumbas sa isang krimen, sapagkat ang impeachment ay hindi naman po usapin lamang ng 
paglabag sa batas, kung hindi paglabag sa tiwalang ipinagkakaloob ng taumbayan.

blocks.normalized_text: iyon naman pong pag-iimbestiga at paghahanap doon sa taong kinausap para pumatay hindi po ito napapaloob sa revised penal code dahil iyon pong pagpaplano ng pagpatay ay hindi naman po pinaparusahan iyan kung hindi nga nagawa subalit doon na po papasok iyong sinasabi nga natin na mayroon pong sinasabi na sa impeachment tinitingnan po at para sa akin nga iyong betrayal of public trust dito po papasok bakit po dahil mayroon po siyang sinumpaang tungkulin doon po mismo sa kaniyang oath of office bilang vice president sinabi niya that protect and defend the constitution execute the laws do justice to everyone and consecrate herself to protect the nation pero doon po sa sinabing execute the laws dito po natin nakikita hindi po execute the laws iyong pag-usap ng papatay that is to execute the president kaya doon po papasok iyong magkaiba na betrayal of public trust
blocks.raw_text: Iyon naman pong pag-iimbestiga at paghahanap doon sa taong kinausap para pumatay, hindi po ito napapaloob sa Revised Penal Code dahil iyon pong pagpaplano ng pagpatay ay hindi naman po pinaparusahan iyan kung hindi nga nagawa. Subalit, doon na po papasok iyong sinasabi nga natin na mayroon pong sinasabi na sa impeachment, tinitingnan po at para sa akin nga, iyong betrayal of public trust, dito po papasok. Bakit po? Dahil mayroon po siyang sinumpaang tungkulin. Doon po mismo sa kaniyang oath of office bilang Vice President, sinabi niya that “protect and defend the Constitution, execute the laws, do justice to everyone, and consecrate herself to protect the nation.” Pero doon po sa sinabing “execute the laws,” dito po natin nakikita, hindi po execute the laws iyong pag-usap ng papatay—that is to execute the President. Kaya doon po papasok iyong magkaiba na betrayal of public trust.

blocks.raw_text: Isn’t that against Republic Act 10867? The statute of power, the act reorganizing and modernizing the National Bureau of Investigation. Sabi dito sa inyong Section 4, “the NBI shall have the following powers and ftmctions: Undertake and investigate the detection of crimes and offenses enumerated under Section 5.” Section 5, may enumeration; human trafficking cases in all airports, extrajudicial or extra-legal killings committed by state’s security forces against media practitioners and activists, killings of justices and judges, violation of Republic Act No. 10175, otherwise known as the Cyber Crime Prevention Act, cases referred by the Inter- Agency Anti-Graft Coordinating Councils, and violations of the Anti-Dummy Law. Tapos, cases involving threats to security or assaults against persons of the President, Vice President, Senate President, Speaker of the House, and Chief Justice of the Supreme Court. Walang congressman, senators, unlike judges, lahat. Transnational crimes, pursuant to existing international agreements, identification of dead/victims in case of mass fatality incidents caused by natural disasters, and violation of commercial, economic, and financial or white-collar crimes... So, exclusively 'yung enumeration. Then, provided, however, if—that the President or Secretary of Justice may direct the NBI to undertake investigation of any crime when public interest so requires.

identification of dead/victims in case of mass fatality incidents caused by natural disasters, and 
violation of commercial, economic, and financial or white-collar crimes... So, exclusively 'yung 
enumeration. Then, provided, however, if—that the President or Secretary of Justice may direct the

blocks.normalized_text: our jurisprudence is very clear a witness must be competent and credible hindi ko na babasahin lahat iyong jurisprudence natin diyan dahil kompleto na iyong record natin diyan but there is also included in our rules rule 132 on the rules of evidence and i ll read it a witness may be impeached by the party against whom he or she was called by contradictory evidence by evidence that his or her general reputation for truth honesty or integrity is bad or by evidence that he or she has made at other times statements inconsistent with his or her present testimony so this is the rules siguro we can well i m reading the rules so we can take notice of this that this is what our rules say and i don t think anyone will contradict that
blocks.raw_text: Our jurisprudence is very clear. A witness must be competent and credible. Hindi ko na babasaHin laHat iyong jurisprudence natin diyan daHil kompleto na iyong record natin diyan. But there is also included in our rules. Rule 132 on the Rules of Evidence, and I’ll read it. “A witness may be impeached by the party against whom he or she was called by contradictory evidence, by evidence that his or her general reputation for truth, honesty, or integrity is bad, or by evidence that he or she has made at other times statements inconsistent with his or her present testimony.” So, this is the Rules, siguro we can well. I’m reading the Rules, so we can take notice of this that this is what our Rules say, and I don’t think anyone will contradict that.
blocks.source_text:
Our jurisprudence is very clear. A witness must be competent and credible. Hindi ko 
na babasaHin laHat iyong jurisprudence natin diyan daHil kompleto na iyong record natin 
diyan. But there is also included in our rules. Rule 132 on the Rules of Evidence, and I’ll read it. 
“A witness may be impeached by the party against whom he or she was called by contradictory 
evidence, by evidence that his or her general reputation for truth, honesty, or integrity is bad, 
or by evidence that he or she has made at other times statements inconsistent with his or 
her present testimony.” So, this is the Rules, siguro we can well. I’m reading the Rules, 
so we can take notice of this that this is what our Rules say, and I don’t think anyone will 
contradict that.

The Presiding Officer suggested that Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) continue her intervention 
during the following day’s proceedings, after the Counsel for the Respondent shall have completed 
the cross-examination of NBI Director Matibag. He added that all other senator-judges would have 
the opportunity to raise their interventions after the cross-examination, redirect, and any re-cross 
examination.

blocks.raw_text: 5. The Presiding Officer announced, as agreed upon by the senator-judges during caucus, that there would be no impeachment trials on Monday, July 27, due to the President’s State of the Nation Address (SONA), and on Tuesday, July 28, to allow the Minority senators to prepare and deliver their traditional contra-SONA; the trial proceedings would resume on Wednesday, July 29, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., after which the Senate would convene in regular session at 4:O0 p.m. He further announced that beginning in August, impeachment proceedings would be held every Monday to Wednesday, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.
blocks.source_text:
5. The Presiding Officer announced, as agreed upon by the senator-judges during caucus, 
that there would be no impeachment trials on Monday, July 27, due to the President’s 
State of the Nation Address (SONA), and on Tuesday, July 28, to allow the Minority 
senators to prepare and deliver their traditional contra-SONA; the trial proceedings 
would resume on Wednesday, July 29, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., after which the 
Senate would convene in regular session at 4:O0 p.m. He further announced that 
beginning in August, impeachment proceedings would be held every Monday to 
Wednesday, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

blocks.normalized_text: 6 the presiding officer also instructed director matibag to return the following day at 2 00 p m to continue his testimony including the respondent s cross-examination any redirect and recross examinations and questions from the senator-judges adjournment of the impeachment trial the presiding officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o clock in the afternoon of the following day
blocks.raw_text: 6. The Presiding Officer also instructed Director Matibag to return the following day at 2:00 p.m. to continue his testimony, including the respondent’s cross-examination, any redirect and recross examinations, and questions from the senator-judges. ADJOURNMENT OF THE IMPEACHMENT TRIAL The Presiding Officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o’clock in the afternoon of the following day.
blocks.source_text:
6. The Presiding Officer also instructed Director Matibag to return the following day at 
2:00 p.m. to continue his testimony, including the respondent’s cross-examination, any 
redirect and recross examinations, and questions from the senator-judges.
ADJOURNMENT OF THE IMPEACHMENT TRIAL
The Presiding Officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o’clock in the 
afternoon of the following day.
